heaviest_rainfall.status: On 09 November 2025, a record rainfall of 47.89mm was measured, indicating an exceptionally wet day.  24.6mm per hour was recorded as falling at times during the 144.8 hour storm.

heaviest_rainrate.status: On 02 September 2025, the rain fell at an exceptional rate, peaking at 34.3mm per hour, the heaviest downpour recorded.  A total of 21.19mm fell over the 15.0 hour storm.

longest_rain.status: The longest rain storm on record lasted 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025 when 47.89mm of rain fell and at one point was falling at 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: Since 11 April 2025, the day with the most lightning strikes is 28 May 2026 when 225 were seen.

storm.status: No stormy weather is anticipated.  The last big storm was recorded on 18 July 2026, lasting 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: We are in the midst of one of the longest dry periods recorded.  This dry period is ranked 1st since records began in 23 September 2024.  The current stretch started on 03 July 2026 and has lasted 20 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 13 January 2026 and lasted for 22 days.

cold_snaps.status: At the moment, it is not a cold snap.  This year has seen 3 cold snaps; typically, 3.7 cold snaps happen every year.

extreme.status: The record temperature difference stands at 27.7°C (-4.5 - 23.2°C), set on 24 April 2026.

heatwaves.status: Right now, a heatwave is in effect, it started on 2026-07-01 and has continued for 22 days.  There has been 3 heatwaves this year; usually, 1.9 occurs annually.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was recorded on 28 June 2026.  The total number of occurrences since 1973 number is only 68.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is south-westerly (217°).  In the UK, a south-westerly wind often means mild and wet weather.

gusts.status: The highest wind gust ever seen is 128.2 mph on 27 January 2026.

run.status: The record wind run remains at 1737 miles from 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The record wind speed remains at 93.7 mph from 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 11%).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in light levels (up 5542 lux).

The past week and month had observable variation in cloud levels (up 1416 m), light levels (up 7526 lux), and dew point temperature (up 1.8°C) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 1188.9m lower than yesterday, 1416.3m lower than the past week, 737.2m lower than the past month.

Yesterday’s cloud levels showed a low of 1829m and a peak of 6260m, averaging 3040m; the week and month recorded warmer averages.
cloud levels.summary: The sky’s high clouds will permit plenty of sunshine.

summary.summary: Inside; hot temperatures are forecast. Outside; the heat will be fierce, likely to feel very uncomfortable in the bright sun. Also, humidity will be quite oppressive and calm and still conditions are expected. Chance of light rain, the sky’s high clouds will permit plenty of sunshine, and sunlight levels will peak at very high intensity. The weather will likely remain the same.

washing.phrase: The conditions are favourable for drying your laundry outside right now but, as always, monitor the situation for changes to the weather.
